title: Fixing Unnecessary Re-renders in a React Memoized Component with Array Equality Issue

statement.markdown: A memoized React functional component is designed to optimize re-renders by comparing the previous and current props. However, the component has an issue with array equality, causing it to re-render unnecessarily when the array items are the same but the array references are different. Fix the bug to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: The input is an object with an array of items
statement.outputFormat: The output is the rendered JSX
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the array items or the array reference change.

constraints.description: The number of items in the array

examples.explanation: The component should render the items as a list
The component should re-render when the array items change
The component should not re-render when the array items are the same but the array reference is different

hints.text: Check the array equality function used in the memoization
Use a library like Lodash to compare array items

editorial.title: Fixing Unnecessary Re-renders in a React Memoized Component with Array Equality Issue
editorial.intuition: The component should only re-render when the array items or the array reference change
editorial.bruteForce: Compare the previous and current props array references and items
editorial.betterSolution: Use a library like Lodash to compare array items
editorial.optimalSolution: Use `JSON.stringify()` to compare array items and references
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the array items or the array reference change
editorial.dryRun: Example run with a sample array
